title: Console Cubby Shelves
summary: A wide rolling console with a row of open vertical cubbies, built from 1x12 boards and trimmed in 1x2.
description:
The dividers, not the box, are what carry the visual weight here — six identical vertical cubbies read as a unit only if they're evenly spaced, so mark all six divider positions before fastening the first one rather than working divider-by-divider and compounding a small spacing error across the whole width. The top divider's bottom edge is toenailed in at an angle rather than face-nailed, since there's no straight-on access to it once the top is already in place.

The trim is genuinely a second pass, not part of the structural build — the source's own advice is to cut trim to the project's actual measurements rather than trust a fixed number, since plywood-adjacent 1x stock varies board to board. Casters on the base turn a heavy, fully-loaded cubby unit into something that can still be pulled out for cleaning behind it.

cutList.note: Recommend cutting to your project's actual measurements.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 30-1/4", one top at 63-1/2", two shelves at 62" and six vertical dividers at 14" from the Pine 1x12. Cut four top and bottom trim at 63-1/2", ten vertical trim at 28", eight shelf trim at 14" and three caster wheel supports at 11-1/2" from the Pine 1x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft Pine 1x12 and eight 8 ft Pine 1x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Fasten through the sides with 2" nails and glue into the shelves. Then fasten the top in place.

steps.title: Add the dividers
steps.body: Mark the proper spacing for all six dividers before fastening any of them. Fasten the bottom dividers in place, fastening through the shelves into the ends of the dividers. Fasten through the top into the top ends of the dividers. To secure the bottom edge of the top divider, toenail at an angle through the bottom corner into the shelf. Use 2" nails and glue throughout.

steps.title: Add the trim
steps.body: Fasten the trim in place with 2" nails and glue, measuring to fit your actual project rather than the plan's numbers — mark the top and bottom trim positions before fastening the vertical trim. The shelf trim sits flush with the top of each shelf.

steps.title: Add the caster supports
steps.body:
Fasten the caster supports to the bottom of the piece with 1-1/4" nails and glue, then attach the casters to these supports.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Wide console with a row of open vertical cubby shelves
